steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses with the transition word "though." The question asks which punctuation correctly sets off "though" between those two clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘shade, though;’: the comma sets off "though" at the end of the first independent clause, and the semicolon correctly separates that clause from the next independent clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘shade, though,’: the comma after "though" leaves the two independent clauses joined only by punctuation, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘shade, though’: without punctuation after "though," the word introduces a dependent clause instead of separating two independent clauses, producing an illogical concession. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘shade; though’: the semicolon before "though" leaves "though deposits..." as a dependent clause rather than a second independent clause. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
